h2h_summary: Egypt and Iran have met only twice in recorded history — a 1975 friendly won by Iran 2-1 and a 2000 friendly that finished 1-1 — giving Iran a narrow edge with one win and one draw.
wc_history_home: Egypt have appeared at only three previous World Cups (1934, 1990, 2018) and have never won a match or advanced beyond the group stage in any of those tournaments.
wc_history_away: Iran have qualified for six World Cups (1978, 1998, 2006, 2014, 2018, 2022) and are making a fourth consecutive appearance, but have never progressed past the group stage in any of those tournaments.
group_context: Group G features Belgium (FIFA rank 8) as heavy favourites to advance alongside Egypt (rank 34), Iran (rank 20), and New Zealand (rank 86); the Egypt vs Iran match is effectively the group's decisive third-place decider, with the winner holding a strong chance of claiming second place and a knockout-round berth.
strengths_home: Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush provide a world-class attacking partnership capable of deciding games individually
Exceptional defensive record in CAF qualifying — seven clean sheets and only two goals conceded across ten unbeaten matches
Tactical flexibility under Hossam Hassan, shifting between 4-2-3-1 and 3-4-1-2 to control games and frustrate opponents
strengths_away: Mehdi Taremi's goal-scoring reliability — 10 goals in 15 qualifiers — makes Iran dangerous even with limited possession
Experienced and disciplined defensive block that conceded very few goals in AFC qualification, comfortable sitting deep and counter-attacking
Strong set-piece threat and high squad cohesion from a domestically-grounded core supplemented by experienced European-based players
team_style_home: Egypt deploy a compact, disciplined 4-2-3-1 with defensive organization as the priority, using an asymmetric double-pivot in build-up and releasing Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush on fast transitions to exploit space behind opposition lines.
team_style_away: Iran line up in a pragmatic 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 mid-to-low block under Amir Ghalenoei, containing opposition in their half before launching rapid counter-attacks and set-piece routines, with the right flank serving as the primary delivery corridor.
recent_news: Egypt is preparing for a decisive Group G showdown with Iran, where a victory would guarantee first place. Mohamed Salah is expected to spearhead Egypt's attack despite injury concerns.
context: Group G features Belgium (FIFA rank 8) as heavy favourites to advance alongside Egypt (rank 34), Iran (rank 20), and New Zealand (rank 86); the Egypt vs Iran match is effectively the group's decisive third-place decider, with the winner holding a strong chance of claiming second place and a knockout-round berth.
